dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7992 IH: Counting Aluminum Net Surplus Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Wittman introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To require the Secretary of the Interior to carry out a study regarding aluminum recycling.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Counting Aluminum Net Surplus Act of 2026 or the CANS Act of 2026.

subsection.text: The Secretary shall carry out an ongoing study to assess the amount of aluminum disposed of in landfills.

paragraph.text: Not later than 3 years after the date of the enactment of this section and triennially thereafter, the Secretary, in consultation with the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency, shall submit to the Committees on Energy and Commerce, Natural Resources, and Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives and the Committees on Commerce, Science, and Transportation and Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate a report regarding the study required under subsection (a).
Each report submitted under paragraph (1) shall include the following information:

subparagraph.text: An estimate of the amount of aluminum disposed of in landfills each year and the associated end-use market of such aluminum.
An estimate of the amount of aluminum disposed of in landfills by industrial waste sites and automotive industry application each year.
An estimate of the amount of aluminum, both in aggregate and per country, exported through foreign scrap purchases, including to foreign entities of concern, each year.
Improvements to collection and sorting technologies needed to ensure aluminum is returned to and used in the supply chain.

clause.text: aluminum can be diverted from landfills; and
aluminum scrap that would otherwise be exported remains in the domestic supply chain.

subparagraph.text: The effect of increased domestic aluminum recycling on—

clause.text: lessening the dependence of the United States on foreign entities of concern for sourcing aluminum; and

paragraph.text: The term aluminum scrap means scrap that is composed primarily of aluminum.
The term foreign entity of concern has the meaning given the term in section 40207(a) of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (42 U.S.C. 18741(a)).
The term Secretary means the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Director of the United States Geological Survey.
